title: App crashes on launch with `SIGABRT` in `dyld __abort_with_payload` on iOS 27.0 Developer Beta
author: achulzhanov
body:
An app using Lottie 4.6.1 crashes immediately on launch when running on a physical device with iOS 27.0 Developer Beta. The same build runs correctly on the iOS 26.5 simulator. The crash occurs before any app code executes, suggesting a dynamic linker failure (missing or changed symbol in the iOS 27 runtime).

Lottie 4.6.1 via SPM
iOS 27.0 Developer Beta crashes
iOS 26.5 simulator works
Xcode 27 beta
macOS 27.0 Developer Beta
iOS 26.0 minimum deployment target

The app crashes before any Swift code runs. The Xcode debugger shows:
- Signal: `SIGABRT`
- Crashed thread: Thread 1 - `dyld __abort_with_payload`
- Memory at the crash: 0KB (process never fully initialized)
- Visible call stack:
```
0  dyld  __abort_with_payload
...
6  dyld  start
```

Workaround: Removing Lottie from SPM dependencies entirely resolves the crash, which strongly suggests the crash originates from Lottie's framework loading. Wrapping usage in `#available(iOS 27, *)` does *not* help, since the crash occurs during dynamic linking before any Swift code runs.

This may be related to an API removed or renamed between iOS 26 and 27. I searched the existing issues and did not find any prior reports of this on iOS 27. Happy to provide more diagnostic information if there is a way to get the full symbolicated stack trace from a dyld-level crash on a beta device.

title: Automatically falling back to Main Thread rendering engine
author: Enjoyworld-AI
body:
I often encounter the warning below. what reason

Encountered Core Animation compatibility issue while setting up animation:
[Shape Layer 1.Polystar 1.Polystar Path 1] Rounded corners support is currently not implemented for polygon items
This animation may have additional compatibility issues, but animation setup was canceled early to avoid wasted work.

Automatically falling back to Main Thread rendering engine. This fallback comes with some additional performance
overhead, which can be reduced by manually specifying that this animation should always use the Main Thread engine.

title: Performance improvement for main-thread NodeRenderSystem
author: ChrisBenua
body: ## Problem Summary In large apps, Swift runtime protocol conformance checks can significantly impact startup time and app responsiveness. This issue documents profiling results and proposes concrete fixes to reduce casting overhead in Lottie's NodeRenderSystem. ## Background: Swift Protocol Casting Costs The first conformance check for any given `(type, protocol)` pair is expensive because the runtime must [scan all protocol conformance type descriptors](https://github.com/swiftlang/swift/blob/0552f3640c2e01d4faeb1e7b86677419446ac8ce/stdlib/public/runtime/ProtocolConformance.cpp#L1091-L1097). After a successful cast, Swift caches the protocol witness table for that `(type, protocol)` pair - but in apps that initialize Lottie animations at startup, repeated casting across many types can still add up to measurable overhead. ## Profiling Results Profiling was performed in our app on iOS Simulator and iPhone 13 (Release build). | Environment | MainThreadAnimationLayer.init total | Time spent in protocol casts | |---------------|-------------------------------------|------------------------------| | iOS Simulator | ~25ms | 17ms (68%) | | iPhone 13 | ~42ms | 31ms (74%) | The majority of this time was spent inside GroupNode.init and initializeNodeTree, as shown below: After applying proposed fixes all overhead from type-casting is gone ## Root Cause There are two categories of protocol casting happening inside the `NodeRenderSystem`: - Explicit casts in ([ItemExtension.swift](https://github.com/airbnb/lottie-ios/blob/master/Sources/Private/MainThread/NodeRenderSystem/Extensions/ItemsExtension.swift#L97-L104)) - `if let pathNode = nodeTree.rootNode as? PathNode { ... }` - `if let renderNode = nodeTree.rootNode as? RenderNode { ... }` - Implicit casts in ([ShapeCompositionLayer.swift](https://github.com/airbnb/lottie-ios/blob/ea35e6a4ec7f443a2b0b69ae97cccf0e946ef4a2/Sources/Private/MainThread/LayerContainers/CompLayers/ShapeCompositionLayer.swift#L27) and [GroupNode.swift](https://github.com/airbnb/lottie-ios/blob/ea35e6a4ec7f443a2b0b69ae97cccf0e946ef4a2/Sources/Private/MainThread/NodeRenderSystem/Nodes/RenderContainers/GroupNode.swift#L100)) - `childKeypaths.append(contentsOf: results.childrenNodes)` ## Proposed Fixes **Fix 1: Replace protocol casts with exhaustive type switches in ItemsExtension.swift** Since `PathNode` and `RenderNode` are internal protocols with a closed, known set of conforming types , we can replace runtime protocol casting with concrete type switches. This avoids conformance scanning entirely. ```swift private func getPathNode(rootNode: AnimatorNode?) -> PathNode? { guard let rootNode else { return nil } switch rootNode { case let node as EllipseNode: return node case let node as PolygonNode: return node case let node as ShapeNode: return node case let node as StarNode: return node case let node as RectangleNode: return node default: return nil } } private func getRenderNode(rootNode: AnimatorNode?) -> RenderNode? { guard let rootNode else { return nil } switch rootNode { case let node as GradientFillNode: return node case let node as StrokeNode: return node case let node as FillNode: return node case let node as GradientStrokeNode: return node default: return nil } } ``` And update `initializeNodeTree` to use these helpers: ```swift if let pathNode = getPathNode(rootNode: nodeTree.rootNode) { nodeTree.paths.append(pathNode.pathOutput) } if let renderNode = getRenderNode(rootNode: nodeTree.rootNode) { nodeTree.renderContainers.append(ShapeRenderLayer(renderer: renderNode.renderer)) } ``` Tradeoffs: This approach violates the Open/Closed Principle. Any new type conforming to `PathNode` or `RenderNode` must also be added to these switch statements. A compile-time exhaustiveness check or a documentation comment on the protocols would help mitigate this risk. **Fix 2: Explicit element-wise boxing to avoid `_arrayForceCast`** Replacing implicit array covariance casts with an explicit `.map { $0 as KeypathSearchable }` causes the compiler to emit a direct existential box operation per element, completely bypassing _arrayForceCast and its per-element conformance checks. ```swift // GroupNode.swift — before var childKeypaths: [KeypathSearchable] = tree.childrenNodes // GroupNode.swift — after var childKeypaths: [KeypathSearchable] = tree.childrenNodes.map { $0 as KeypathSearchable } ``` ```swift // ShapeCompositionLayer.swift — before childKeypaths.append(contentsOf: results.childrenNodes) // ShapeCompositionLayer.swift — after childKeypaths.append(contentsOf: results.childrenNodes.map { $0 as KeypathSearchable }) ``` Why this works: The explicit as `KeypathSearchable` cast on a value already known to be a concrete conforming type is resolved at compile time. The `.map` introduces a small allocation, but this is far cheaper than repeated conformance table scanning.

title: Xcode Cloud frequently fails to resolve package due to XCFramework download failure - xcodebuild: error: Could not resolve package dependencies
author: rr-james-hickman
body:
We install Lottie via SPM using the SPM repo: `https://github.com/airbnb/lottie-spm.git`
Our team sees this issue occur very frequently in Xcode Cloud:
```
xcodebuild: error: Could not resolve package dependencies: failed downloading 'https://github.com/airbnb/lottie-ios/releases/download/4.4.3/Lottie-Xcode-15.2.xcframework.zip' which is required by binary target 'Lottie': downloadError("A server with the specified hostname could not be found."). fatalError
```

I've just updated the version to latest `4.6.0` and will see if it keeps occurring, but wondering if this is something others are experiencing when using the `lottie-spm` repo?

If we still see the failure with the updated version (`4.6.0`) then I will try switching to the main repo instead of the SPM one and see if that fixes the issue. But just wanted to bring this to your attention to see if you were aware of it and could possibly fix.

Thanks!

title: Fatal crash in Core Animation renderer: "Size must be non-zero before an animation can be played" (TransformAnimations.swift:148)
author: mpreikschat
body:
# Fatal crash in Core Animation renderer with nested pre-compositions (SVGLength assertion)

## Description

When playing certain Lottie animations (exported from Jitter) that contain deeply nested pre-compositions, zero-scale layers, and animated transforms, the Core Animation rendering engine crashes with:

```
Fatal error: Size must be non-zero before an animation can be played
```

The crash occurs regardless of playback mode — even `.paused` triggers it. Switching to `.mainThread` rendering resolves the issue.

## Environment

- **lottie-ios version:** 4.6.0 (also reproduced on 4.5.2)
- **Xcode:** 26.3
- **iOS Simulator:** 18.6
- **iOS Device:** 26.3
- **Integration:** SwiftUI using `LottieView`

## Stack Trace

```
Lottie/TransformAnimations.swift:148: Fatal error: Size must be non-zero before an animation can be played

*** Assertion failure in +[SVGLength pixelsPerInchForCurrentDevice], SVGLength.m:238

#0  Swift.assertionFailure
#1  closure #1 in default argument 1 of LottieLogger.init(assert:assertionFailure:warn:info:)
#2  LottieLogger.assertionFailure(_:fileID:line:)
#3  closure #1 in CALayer.addAnchorPointAnimation(from:context:)
#4  CALayer.defaultAnimation<Lottie.LottieVector3D, CGPoint>(for:keyframe...)
#5  CALayer.addAnimation<Lottie.LottieVector3D, CGPoint>(for:keyframe...)
#6  CALayer.addAnchorPointAnimation(from:context:)
#7  CALayer.addTransformAnimations(for:context:)
#8  BaseCompositionLayer.setupLayerAnimations(context:)
#9  PreCompLayer.setupAnimations(context:)
#10 BaseAnimationLayer.setupAnimations(context:)
...
#41 CoreAnimationLayer.setupAnimation(for:)
#42 CoreAnimationLayer.display()
```

## Animation JSON Characteristics

The problematic animation JSON (exported from [[Jitter](https://jitter.video/)](https://jitter.video)) has:

- 13 assets with deeply nested pre-compositions (4-5 levels)
- ~150+ layers with deep parenting chains
- A zero-scale layer (`"s": [0, 0]`) on a placeholder asset
- A 1×1 pixel embedded PNG image asset
- Animated scale starting at `[50, 50]` and opacity starting at `0`
- Lottie format v5.7.4

Simple animations (e.g., bouncing dots with basic shape layers and no pre-compositions) work fine with the Core Animation renderer.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Create a SwiftUI view using `LottieView` with default (Core Animation) rendering
2. Load an animation with the characteristics described above
3. The app crashes immediately — even with `.paused` playback mode

```swift
// Crashes
LottieView(animation: LottieAnimation.named("problematic_animation"))
    .playbackMode(.paused)

// Works
LottieView(animation: LottieAnimation.named("problematic_animation"))
    .configure { view in
        view.configuration = LottieConfiguration(renderingEngine: .mainThread)
    }
    .playbackMode(.paused)
```

## Workaround

Switching to the Main Thread rendering engine avoids the crash:

```swift
.configure { animationView in
    animationView.configuration = LottieConfiguration(renderingEngine: .mainThread)
}
```

## Expected Behavior

The Core Animation renderer should either:
- Handle zero-scale layers and nested pre-compositions without crashing
- Gracefully skip layers that would result in zero-size transforms
- Fall back to main thread rendering for unsupported layer configurations

## Additional Context

- The crash originates in `CALayer.addAnchorPointAnimation` when computing transforms for nested pre-composition layers
- The `SVGLength pixelsPerInchForCurrentDevice` assertion suggests an issue with unit conversion during transform calculation
- The animation plays correctly on web-based Lottie players (lottie-web)
- The `.mainThread` renderer handles the same animation without issues

title: Enable ColorValueProvider support for Text Layer base color
author: ciss1995
body:
Currently, it is not possible to dynamically change the base text color of a Text Layer using setValueProvider(_:forKeypath:).

TextCompositionLayer renders text using a TextDocument (which contains a statically parsed fillColorData), but it does not expose this color as a targetable NodeProperty via the KeypathSearchable protocol.

In TextCompositionLayer.swift
```
var keypathProperties: [String: AnyNodeProperty] {
  [:] // Empty! Base text color is untargetable
}
```
This acts differently from Shape Layers, where FillNode explicitly exposes its color:

```
// FillNode.swift
var keypathProperties: [String: AnyNodeProperty] {
  return ["Color": color]
}
```

Proposed Solution:
Modify TextCompositionLayer to:
1/ Create a NodeProperty<LottieColor> for the fill color.
2/ Expose it in keypathProperties (e.g., as "Color" or "Fill Color").
3/ Use this property's value in displayContentsWithFrame if it has an active provider.

title: Crash: Double-free in AnimationContext when removing animations (Core Animation engine)
author: ed-huang-jp
body:
### Summary
App crashes with ___BUG_IN_CLIENT_OF_LIBMALLOC_POINTER_BEING_FREED_WAS_NOT_ALLOCATED when AnimationContext is deallocated. This occurs due to a race condition between Swift ARC and Core Animation's memory management when removing animations.

### Crash Stack Trace
```
Crashed: com.apple.main-thread

0  libsystem_kernel.dylib         0xb1dc __pthread_kill + 8
1  libsystem_pthread.dylib        0x7c60 pthread_kill + 268
2  libsystem_c.dylib              0x770ac abort + 124
3  libsystem_malloc.dylib         0x16d70 malloc_vreport + 892
4  libsystem_malloc.dylib         0x169e8 malloc_report + 64
5  libsystem_malloc.dylib         0x11594 ___BUG_IN_CLIENT_OF_LIBMALLOC_POINTER_BEING_FREED_WAS_NOT_ALLOCATED + 32
6  libswiftCore.dylib             0x39978c _swift_release_dealloc + 56
7  libswiftCore.dylib             0x39a700 bool swift::RefCounts<swift::RefCountBitsT<(swift::RefCountInlinedness)1>>::doDecrementSlow<(swift::PerformDeinit)1>(swift::RefCountBitsT<(swift::RefCountInlinedness)1>, unsigned int) + 152
8  App                            0x241498 destroy for AnimationContext + 4338406552
```
